goal: Build a high-speed belt stress test: same as CP-01 but conveyor velocity 0.5 (vs 0.2) — apparent speed ~1.5 m/s with stick-slip multiplier. Tests cuRobo controller's ability to plan + execute pick under faster cube motion. Industrial relevance: high-throughput conveyor lines.
thoughts:
Six high-speed-belt specifics for CP-22 (extends CP-01 — same scene, faster belt):
1) surface_velocity=[0.5, 0, 0] (vs CP-01's 0.2). With ~3× stick-slip multiplier, apparent cube speed ~1.5 m/s. cuRobo handler must plan picks in ~0.5s window before cube exits sensor zone.
2) Sensor zone 0.06×0.06×0.06m at x=0.4. At 1.5 m/s a cube transits in 0.04s — handler triggers belt-pause IMMEDIATELY on sensor-overlap detection.
3) Controller's settling state (8 ticks ≈ 0.13s) lets cube come to rest after belt pause. Belt high speed means more residual cube velocity → more settling time may be needed.
4) Cube spacing 0.30m on conveyor. Higher belt speed → cubes arrive at sensor zone every ~0.20s. Robot cycle ~5-7s; queue builds up. Cubes 3-4 may collide while waiting.
5) Same physics gotchas as CP-01: CPU dynamics, rubber on cubes, sleepThreshold=0. High velocity stresses these — sleepThreshold matters because cubes may briefly stop mid-belt at speed transitions.
6) Form-gate verifies bridging works (sensor + belt). Function-gate is the real test of high-speed robustness — likely lower delivery rate than CP-01's 4/4.
code:
# DomeLight + Ground + Cell + Table
create_prim(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", prim_type="DomeLight")
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", attr_name="inputs:intensity", value=1000.0)
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Ground", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, 0, -0.5], scale=[20, 20, 1])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Ground", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Cell", prim_type="Xform")
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Table", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, 0, 0.375], scale=[1.0, 0.5, 0.375])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Table", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

set_physics_scene_config(config={"enable_gpu_dynamics": False, "broadphase_type": "MBP"})

robot_wizard(
    robot_name="franka_panda",
    dest_path="/World/Franka",
    position=[0, 0, 0.75],
    orientation=[0.7071068, 0, 0, 0.7071068],
)

# HIGH SPEED conveyor — 0.5 m/s nominal (vs CP-01's 0.2)
create_conveyor(
    prim_path="/World/ConveyorBelt",
    position=[0.0, 0.4, 0.78],
    size=[3.0, 0.4, 0.05],
    surface_velocity=[0.5, 0, 0],
)

for i, x in enumerate([-1.4, -1.1, -0.8, -0.5]):
    path = f"/World/Cube_{i+1}"
    create_prim(prim_path=path, prim_type="Cube", position=[x, 0.4, 0.835], size=0.05)
    for api in ("PhysicsRigidBodyAPI", "PhysicsCollisionAPI", "PhysicsMassAPI", "PhysxRigidBodyAPI"):
        apply_api_schema(prim_path=path, schema_name=api)

bulk_set_attribute(
    prim_paths=[f"/World/Cube_{i+1}" for i in range(4)],
    attr="physxRigidBody:sleepThreshold",
    value=0.0,
)
for path in [f"/World/Cube_{i+1}" for i in range(4)]:
    apply_physics_material(prim_path=path, material_name="rubber")

create_bin(prim_path="/World/Bin", position=[0, -0.4, 0.75], size=[0.3, 0.3, 0.15])

add_proximity_sensor(sensor_path="/World/PickSensor", position=[0.4, 0.4, 0.835], size=[0.06, 0.06, 0.06])

setup_pick_place_controller(
    robot_path="/World/Franka",
    target_source="curobo",
    sensor_path="/World/PickSensor",
    belt_path="/World/ConveyorBelt",
    source_paths=[f"/World/Cube_{i+1}" for i in range(4)],
    destination_path="/World/Bin",
    planning_obstacles=["/World/Table", "/World/ConveyorBelt", "/World/Bin"],
)
extension_notes: CP-22 stress-tests cuRobo's planner cycle time under fast belt conditions. Industrial use: high-throughput sortation lines. Future variants: even faster (1.0 m/s nominal), variable-speed (slow on inspect, fast on transit), with multi-robot pipeline.
failure_modes: Belt too fast → cube exits sensor zone before pause + settle complete → cube travels past pick point. Drop velocity to 0.3-0.4 if 0.5 fails.
Cubes collide on belt due to short spacing + fast speed. 0.30m spacing at 1.5 m/s gives 0.20s gap; tight but manageable.
Robot cycle too long for belt pace → cubes pile up at downstream end of belt. Acceptable for canonical demo.
Same physics gotchas as CP-01: GPU dynamics off, sleepThreshold=0, rubber on cubes.
verified_status: build-spec-2026-05-08; form-gate verification pending; function-gate likely lower delivery rate than CP-01 due to high speed
